name: 1972 Stock (LDN)
desc: 1972 Stock is a subway EMU built by Metro-Cammell for London's Deep Tube that entered service on the Bakerloo Line. They were refurbrished twice: in 1991 by Tickford, and in 2016 by Acton Works. Cost is an estimate.

name: 1973 Stock (LDN)
desc: 1973 Stock is a subway EMU built by Metro-Cammell for London's Deep Tube that entered service on the Piccadilly Line. They were refurbrished in 1996 by Bombardier Prorail. Cost is an estimate.

name: 1992 Stock (LDN)
desc: 1992 Stock is a subway EMU built by BREL and ABB for London's Deep Tube that entered service on the Central Line, and is now also used on the Waterloo & City Line. Waterloo & City Line sets were refurbrished in 2006, while Central were in 2019. Cost is an estimate.

name: 1996 Stock (LDN)
desc: 1996 Stock is a subway EMU built by Alstom for London's Deep Tube that entered service on the Jubilee Line.

name: 2009 Stock (LDN)
desc: 2009 Stock is a subway EMU built by Bombardier for London's Deep Tube that entered service on the Victoria Line.

name: 2024 Stock (LDN)
desc: 2024 Stock is a subway EMU built by Siemens as part of their Inspiro family. It will enter service around early 2027 on the Picadilly Line, and potentially later the Central, Waterloo & City, and Bakerloo lines.

name: 7000 Series (WSH)
desc: The 7000-series is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for Washington DC's Subway that entered service in 2015.

name: 8000 Series (WSH)
desc: The 8000-series is a subway EMU built by Hitachi for Washington DC's Subway that will enter service in 2028. Acceleration is a guesstimate.
The Metrovagonmash 81-717/81-714 is a Soviet era heavy metro EMU that entered service in 1977. It remains in use across the former USSR.

name: 9000 Series (Lima)
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. The Series 9000 is a heavy metro variant built for the Lima Metro that entered service in 2016. Acceleration is guesstimated.

name: A Series (PER)
desc: The A-Series is the first generation of EMU's built for Perth by Walker (now Downer) and entered service in 1991. Today they operate mainly on Perth's heritage Lines.

name: A/B Sets (SYD)
desc: The A and B sets / Waratahs are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by Downer Rail and CRRC that entered service in 2011.

name: A650 (LA)
desc: The A650 is a subway EMU built by Breda/AnsaldoBreda for the LA Metro's heavy rail lines that entered service in 1993.

name: ADtranz M4
desc: The AdTranz M4 is an EMU used on the L line of the Philadelphia Subway. Many of these stats are estimates due to lack of data.

name: ALP45-BMLC
desc: The Bombardier ALP-45 is a dual-mode locomotive that first entered service in 2010. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier MultiLevel Coaches in their trailer configuration.

name: ALP46-BMLC
desc: The Bombardier ALP-45 is an electric locomotive that first entered service in 2001. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier MultiLevel Coaches in their trailer configuration.

name: Altéo (PAR)
desc: The MI 2N Altéo is a double-decker EMU built by Alstom as part of their X'Trapolis family that has been in service on the French RER's line A since 1997. They were refurbrished from 2019-2021. Cost is an estimate

name: AM4-M4
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. The AM4-M4 is an Automated Metro variant built for the Budapest Metro's M4 that entered service in 2014.

name: AM5-M2
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. The AM5-M2 is a heavy metro variant built for the Budapest Metro's M2 that entered service in 2012.

name: AnsaldoBreda DM Generic
desc: The Hitachi Rail Italy Driverless Metro (formerly AnsaldoBreda) is a fully autonomous EMU family used across the globe. This is a generic model designed to allow a wider variety of cars with one model.

name: AnsaldoBreda DM-7
desc: The Hitachi Rail Italy Driverless Metro (formerly AnsaldoBreda) is a fully autonomous EMU family used across the globe. This specific model is based on those used by Lima's Metro since 2017.

name: AnsaldoBreda DM-L4
desc: The Hitachi Rail Italy Driverless Metro (formerly AnsaldoBreda) is a fully autonomous EMU family used across the globe. This specific model is based on those used by the Honolulu Skyline since 2023. Cost is adjusted down bc Hawaii.

name: AnsaldoBreda DM-L6
desc: The Hitachi Rail Italy Driverless Metro (formerly AnsaldoBreda) is a fully autonomous EMU family used across the globe. This specific model is based on those used by Rome's Metro since 2014.

name: AnsaldoBreda DM-M
desc: The Hitachi Rail Italy Driverless Metro (formerly AnsaldoBreda) is a fully autonomous EMU family used across the globe. This specific model is based on those used by the New Taipei metro since 2020.

name: AnsaldoBreda DM-S
desc: The Hitachi Rail Italy Driverless Metro (formerly AnsaldoBreda) is a fully autonomous EMU family used across the globe. This specific model is based on those used by Copenhagen, Brescia, and Milan since 2013.

name: Artic MLNRV-3
desc: The Arctic MLNRV-3 is an articulated LRV built by Škoda for Helsinki's tram network that entered service in 2013. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: Artic X54
desc: The Arctic X54 is an articulated LRV built by Škoda for Helsinki's light rail network that entered service in 2021. It is expandable. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: Avenio (CPH)
desc: The Siemens Avenio is a low-floor LRV family that has been in service since 2009. This is the version used in Copenhagen.

name: Azur (MTL)
desc: The MPM-10 (Azur) is a rubber-tire vehicle built by Bombardier and Alstom for the Montreal Metro that entered service in 2016.

name: B 82500 BIBI
desc: The B 82500 'BIBI' is a commuter rail double-decker dual-mode MU built by Bombardier that entered service in 2007. It operates on the Transilien's P line.

name: B Series (PER)
desc: The B-series was built by Downer for the Mandurah/Yanchep Lines and entered service in 2004. Today they operate on all Perth routes.

name: BART D/E
desc: The Fleet of the Future, classified as D and E series, is an electric EMU built by Bombardier/Alstom as a custom order for BART. They entered service in 2018. I genuinely could not find capacity numbers so I guesstimated.

name: BHEV MX
desc: The BHEV MX (and MXA) are commuter rail EMUs in service since the 70s in Budapest. They were made by East German manufacturer LEW Hennigsdorf. Cost is a guesstimate.

name: BL Series 5 (BOS)

name: BR Class 150

name: BR Class 158/159

name: BR Class 165

name: BR Class 171/172
desc: The Turbostar (BR 171/172) is a commuter rail DMU built by Bombardier for GTR that entered service in 2003. Cost is an estimate.

name: BR Class 345

name: BR Class 350

name: BR Class 357

name: BR Class 360

name: BR Class 360-2

name: BR Class 375

name: BR Class 379

name: BR Class 458

name: BR Class 700

name: BR Class 701

name: BR Class 710

name: BR Class 720

desc: The Alstom Aventra is a family of EMUs in service in the UK since 2015. This model is the EMU labeled Class 730 as used by London Northwestern Railway.

name: C151A/B/C (SGP)

It comes in 3 series - A, B, and C, although they seem to be identical outside of time of construction, so they have been merged here.

name: C810A (SGP)
desc: The C810A is an automated people mover built by Mitsubishi for Singapore's LRT that entered service in 2003 on the Sengkang and Punggol Lines. It can be run as singular cars IRL, but game restrictions force it to be 2 cars only. Cost is a guesstimate based on the C810D.

name: C810D (SGP)
desc: The C810D is an automated people mover built by Mitsubishi for Singapore's LRT that entered service in 2025 on the Sengkang and Punggol Lines. It can be run as singular cars IRL, but game restrictions force it to be 2 cars only.

name: Citadis 302

name: Citadis 302 N

name: Citadis Spirit-3

name: Citadis Spirit-4
desc: The Citadis Spirit is a family of articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Alstom that have been in service in Canada since 2019. This is the 4-segment version.

name: Citadis Spirit-5

name: Combino (AMS)
desc: The Combino family are articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Siemens that have been in service across the world since the 90s. This specific model is based on the Amsterdam Metro's G14. Capacity is artifically increased due to game processing limitations. Acceleration is guesstimated.

name: Coradia iLINT 54
desc: The Coradia LINT is a series of Diesel (and some Hydrogen) MUs built by Alstom since 1993 that are in usage across the world. The number roughly corresponds to vehicle length. Hydrogen LINTs are called iLINT. iLINT Cost is adjusted down by 2 million for balance.

name: Coradia LINT 27
desc: The Coradia LINT is a series of Diesel (and some Hydrogen) MUs built by Alstom since 1993 that are in usage across the world. The number roughly corresponds to vehicle length. Hydrogen LINTs are called iLINT. iLINT Cost is adjusted down by 2 million for balance.

name: Coradia LINT 41
desc: The Coradia LINT is a series of Diesel (and some Hydrogen) MUs built by Alstom since 1993 that are in usage across the world. The number roughly corresponds to vehicle length. Hydrogen LINTs are called iLINT. iLINT Cost is adjusted down by 2 million for balance.

name: CQ312 (ATL)
desc: The CQ 312 is a subway EMU built by Stadler for the Atlanta metro which entered service in 2001. Cost is an estimate.

name: CQ400 (ATL)
desc: The CQ 400 is a subway EMU built by Stadler for the Atlanta metro which will enter service in 2026.

name: CR151 (SGP)
desc: The CR151 is an EMU being built by CRRC for Singapore's MRT that will enter service in 2030 on the Cross Island Line. It initially will come in 6 car sets but is expanable to up to 8. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: CRRC HR4000 (LA)
desc: The HR4000 is a subway EMU built by CRRC for the LA Metro's heavy rail lines that entered service in 2024. Acceleration is guessimated.

name: CTA 3200 Series
desc: The 3200 series is a subway EMU built by Morrison-Knudsen and Mafersa for the Chicago L that entered service in 1992.

name: CTA 5000 Series
desc: The 5000 series is a subway EMU built by Bombardier for the Chicago L that entered service in 2011.

name: Cuautitlán-Buenavista
desc: The Cuautitlán-Buenavista is a commuter rail EMU built by CAF used on Mexico City's Tren Suburbano that entered service in 2008.

name: D Sets (NSW)
desc: The D sets / Mariyung are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by Hyundai Rotem, UGC, and Mitsubishi that entered service in 2024.

name: DBAG 474 (HAM)
desc: The Class 474 is an S-Bahn EMU built by ADtranz/Bombardier and Linke-Hoffman-Busch/Alstom for the Hamburg S-Bahn that entered service in the 1990s. Cost is an estimate

name: DBAG 481 (BER)
desc: The DB Class 481 is an EMU built by AEG, ADtranz, and DWA for the Berlin S-Bahn that entered service in 1996

name: DBAG 483 (BER)
desc: The DB Class 483/484 is an EMU built by Stadler and Siemens for the Berlin S-Bahn that entered service in 2021.

name: DBAG 490 (HAM)
desc: The Class 490 is an S-Bahn EMU built by Bombardier for the Hamburg S-Bahn that entered service in 2018.

name: Desiro Classic (ATH)
desc: The Siemens Desiro Classic is a family of metro DMUs and EMUs in service across the world since 1999. This model is the EMU used by Athens commuter rail. Cost is an estimate.

name: Desiro Classic (SD)
desc: The Siemens Desiro Classic is a family of metro DMUs and EMUs in service across the world since 1999. This model is the DMU used by San Diego's Sprinter service.

name: DLR B07
desc: The B07 is Automated Metro rolling stock for London's Docklands Light Railway, built by Bombardier. Cost is an estimate.

name: DM30-C3 (LIRR)
desc: The DM30AC-C3 is a commuter rail Diesel train that entered service in 1993, with coach cars manufacured by Kawasaki powered by an EMD manufactured Diesel locomotive. Cost is an estimate.

name: DT4
desc: The DT4 is a subway EMU built by ABB and Alstom for the Hamburg U-Bahn that entered service in 1998.

name: DT5
desc: The DT5 is a subway EMU built by Bombardier and Alstom for the Hamburg U-Bahn that entered service in 2012.

name: DT6-A
desc: The DT6 is a subway EMU built by Alstom for the Hamburg U-Bahn that are planned to enter service in 2028. This is the automatic version for the new U5 line. Acceleration is an estimate.

name: DT6-F
desc: The DT6 is a subway EMU built by Alstom for the Hamburg U-Bahn that are planned to enter service in 2028. This is the manual version for the existing U1-U4 lines. Acceleration is an estimate.

name: Éole (PAR)
desc: The Z 22500 Éole is a commuter rail double-decker EMU built by ANF and Alstom that operates on the RER E Line. It entered service in 1996. Cost is an estimate.

name: ES/IC5 (DSB)
desc: The ES (also known as the IC5) is a regional / intercity EMU built by Alstom based off their Coradia Stream for Danish State Railways. It will enter service in 2027.

name: ET 2010
desc: The Flexity Swift is a series of articulated tram/LRT/light metro vehicles produced by Bombardier/Alstom that have been in service for decades around the world. This is the ET 2010 which was built for Karlsruhe and entered service in 2013.

name: F Series (BER)
desc: The Series F is a large-profile series of EMU used by the Berlin U-Bahn manufacturered by a variety of German corporations from 1973 to 1994. This is based on the later F84-92 models. Their acceleration is very high. Cost is an estimate.

name: F59-BBLC
desc: The EMD F59PH is a line of diesel locomotives that first entered service in the 1990s. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier BiLevel Coaches.

name: FE-10 (MXC)
desc: The FE-10 is a steel-wheeled model of EMU built by CAF used on the Mexico City Metro, first used in 2012.

name: Flexity Berlin L
desc: The Flexity Berlin is an articulated low-floor LRV produced by Bombardier/Alstom as a derivative of their Flexity family. It entered service in 2009. This is the longer 7 segment version.

name: Flexity Berlin S
desc: The Flexity Berlin is an articulated low-floor LRV produced by Bombardier/Alstom as a derivative of their Flexity family. It entered service in 2009. This is the shorter 5 segment version. Capacity is artificially higher due to game processing requirements.

name: Flexity Freedom-3
desc: The Flexity Freedom is a family of articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Bombardier/Alstom that have been in service in Canada since 2019. This is the 3 segment version which has never been ordered, so the cost is just a theory.

name: Flexity Freedom-5
desc: The Flexity Freedom is a family of articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Bombardier/Alstom that have been in service in Canada since 2019. This is the 5 segment version.

name: Flexity Freedom-7
desc: The Flexity Freedom is a family of articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Bombardier/Alstom that have been in service in Canada since 2019. This is the 7 segment version used in Edmonton.

name: Flexity Outlook (TOR)
desc: The Flexity Outlook is an articulated low-floor light-rail vehicle built by Bombardier for the Toronto Streetcar that has been in service since 2014. Toronto got a custom model due to their unique gauge.

name: Flexity Swift LF
desc: The Flexity Swift is a series of articulated tram/LRT/light metro vehicles produced by Bombardier/Alstom that have been in service for decades around the world. This is a genericized version of the low-floor models.

name: FLIRT Akku
desc: The FLIRT Akku is a commuter rail battery-capable EMU family built by Stadler for railways across the world in operation since 2023. It operates on battery power for up to 150 km when electric is unavailable. It is generally in 2 car sets, but it can use trailer cars to hit any number of cars desired.

name: Francilien-7 (PAR)
desc: The Z 50000 Francilien is a commuter rail double-decker EMU built by Bombardier that entered service in 2009. This is the 7 car variant seen on Transilien's J and L lines.

name: Francilien-8 (PAR)
desc: The Z 50000 Francilien is a commuter rail double-decker EMU built by Bombardier that entered service in 2009. This is the 8 car variant seen on Transilien's E, H, K, and P lines.

name: Generation 2 (ATH)
desc: The 2nd Generation of Athens lines 2 and 3 rolling stock is a heavy metro dual-mode EMU built by Hyundai, Mitsubishi, and Knorr-Bremse that entered service in 2003. Cost is a guesstimate.

name: Generation 3 (ATH)
desc: The 3rd Generation of Athens lines 2 and 3 rolling stock is a heavy metro EMU built by Siemens and Hyundai that entered service in 2014.

name: GT6-70D/N
desc: The GT6-70D/N is an articulated LRV built by DUEWAG and Siemens based off of the 100C/2S for Karlsruhe that entered service in the late 90s/early 2000s. Capacity is artifically increased due to game limitations.

name: GT8-100C/2S
desc: The GT8-100C/2S is an articulated LRV built by DUEWAG and ABB for the the Karlsruhe Light Rail that entered service in the early 1990s.

name: GT8-100D/2S-M
desc: The GT8-100D/2S-M is an articulated LRV built by DUEWAG and Siemens based off of the 100C/2S for the the Karlsruhe Light Rail that entered service in the late 90s/early 2000s.

name: GT8-70D/N
desc: The GT8-70D/N is an articulated LRV built by DUEWAG and Siemens based off of the 100C/2S for Karlsruhe that entered service in the late 90s/early 2000s.

name: GTW 2/6 (NJT)
desc: This is a variant of the Stadler GTW 2/6 which is one of the rare and bizarre Diesel Light Rail Vehicles and is exclusively in service on NJT's River Line. Details are scarce so the acceleration and braking are guesstimates.

name: GTW 2/6 (SF)
desc: This is a variant of the Stadler GTW 2/6 which is one of the rare and bizarre Diesel Light Rail Vehicles and is in service in Austin and San Francisco.

name: H Series (BER)
desc: The Series H is a large-profile type of EMU used by the Berlin U-Bahn manufacturered by ADtranz/Bombardier and ABB Henschel. They entered service in 1995. Cost is an estimate.

name: H Sets (NSW)
desc: The H sets / OSCARs are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for CityRail/NSW TrainLink by UGL Rail that entered service in 2006. Cost and acceleration are guesstimates.

name: HHP8-BMLC
desc: The Bombardier/Alstom HHP-8 is an electric locomotive that first entered service in 2002. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier MultiLevel Coach (aka Adessia Coach) cars, as used by MARC.

name: Highliner 2
desc: The Highliner II is a bi-level commuter rail EMU built by Nippon Sharyo for Chicago's Metra that entered service in 2005.

name: Hitachi Baltimore
desc: This is an unnamed metro EMU built by Hitachi Rail for Baltimore that entered service in 2026. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: Hitachi Miami
desc: This is an unnamed series of heavy metro trains built by Hitachi Rail for the Miami Metrorail that entered service in 2017. Acceleration is guesstimated.

name: Hitachi Rome
desc: This is an unnamed series of trains built by Hitachi for the Rome Metro Line B that will enter service in 2026. Acceleration is guesstimated.

name: Hitachi Toronto
desc: Hitachi Rail is currently producing a series of unnamed metro cars for the Toronto Subway's new Ontario Line. Cost is a guesstimate, as is acceleration, while capacity and dimensions are estimated from preliminary design. It is expected to enter service around 2031.

name: HK Series (BER)
desc: The Series HK is a small-profile type of EMU used by the Berlin U-Bahn manufacturered by ADtranz/Bombardier from 2005-2006. Cost is an estimate.

name: IC4 (DSB)
desc: The IC4 is a regional / intercity DMU built by AnsaldoBreda for DSB and also one for Muammar Gaddafi that entered full service in 2012.

name: IÉ 22000
desc: The IÉ Class 22000 is an Irish DMU used for both commuter rail and intercity services. It was built by Hyundai Rotem and entered service in 2007. Acceleration is guesstimated. Cost is an estimate.

name: IÉ 29000
desc: The IÉ Class 29000 is an Irish commuter rail DMU built by CAF that entered service in 2003. Acceleration is guesstimated. Cost is an estimate.

name: IK Series (BER)
desc: The Series IK is a small-profile type of EMU used by the Berlin U-Bahn manufacturered by Stadler based off their Tango family. Using bridges, some variants can operate on large profile routes. They entered service in 2015.

name: Innovia APM
desc: The Innovia APM is an automated people mover built by Bombardier that is mainly used in airports. IRL it can run in sets of 1 but game processing requirements mean its 2 minimum.

name: Innovia APM C801A
desc: The C801A is an automated people mover built by Bombardier for Singapore's LRT that entered service in 2014 on the Bukit Panjang Line. They are part of the Innovia People Mover family (100 Series). It can be run as singular cars IRL, but game restrictions force it to be 2 cars only.  Cost is a guesstimate based on the C810D.

name: Innovia APM C801B
desc: The C801B is an automated people mover built by Alstom and CRRC for Singapore's LRT that entered service in 2024 on the Bukit Panjang Line. They are part of the Innovia People Mover family (300 Series). It can be run as singular cars IRL, but game restrictions force it to be 2 cars only.  Cost is a guesstimate based on the C810D.

name: Inspiro (SYD)
desc: The Siemens Inspiro is a family of metro EMUs that have been in service around the world since 2013. This is the automated version on order by the Sydney Metro which is expected to enter service in 2027.

name: IST M1
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the light metro stock that entered service in 1988 on the M1, as made by ABB. Acceleration is a guesstimate. Cost is an estimate.

name: IST M11
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the high speed heavy metro stock that entered service in 2023 on the M11, as made by CRRC. Acceleration and vehicle length are a guesstimate.

name: IST M12
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the automated metro stock that will enter service on the M12, as made by Hyundai Rotem. Acceleration and vehicle length are a guesstimate.

name: IST M2 2008
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the heavy metro stock that entered service in 2008 on the M2, as made by Hyundai. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: IST M2 2017
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the heavy metro stock that entered service in 2017 on the M2, as made by Hyundai. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: IST M3
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the heavy metro stock that entered service in 2009 on the M3 and M9, as made by Alstom. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: IST M4
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the heavy metro stock that entered service in 2010 on the M4, as made by CAF. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: IST M5
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the Automated Metro stock that entered service in 2017 on the M5, as made by CAF and Mitsubishi. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: IST M6
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the heavy metro stock that entered service in 1999 on the M2 that now mainly serves the M6, as made by Alstom. Acceleration is a guesstimate. Cost is an estimate.

name: IST M7/M8
desc: The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the Automated Metro stock that entered service in 2020 on the M7 and M8, as made by Hyundai. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: J Series (BER)
desc: The Series J is a large-profile type of EMU used by the Berlin U-Bahn manufacturered by Stadler. They entered service in 2025.

name: J Sets (NSW)
desc: The J sets / Hunters are commuter rail DMUs built for Sydney Trains by UGL Rail that entered service in 2006. Acceleration and cost are guesstimates. Capacity is increased due to game limits.

name: J151 (SGP)
desc: The J151 is an EMU being built by Hyundai Rotem for Singapore's MRT that will enter service in 2028 on the Jurong Region Line. They come in 3 car sets, but are expandable to 4 cars long. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: JK Series (BER)
desc: The Series JK is a small-profile type of EMU used by the Berlin U-Bahn manufacturered by Stadler. They entered service in 2025.

name: K Sets (SYD)
desc: The K sets are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by A Goninan & Co that entered service in 1981. Cost and acceleration are guesstimates.

name: K-1T
desc: The K-1T is a low-floor articulated LRV built by Tatra-Yug that entered service in 2020. Acceleration is a guesstimate. Capacity is increased artificially due to game processing requirements. Cost is an estimate.

name: Kinki Sharyo SLRV
desc: The Kinki Sharyo Super Light Rail Vehicle (SLRV) is an articulated LRV built for Dallas' DART that entered service in 1996. They are Super LRVs, not Super Light RVs.

name: KISS
desc: The Stadler KISS is a family of bilevel EMU commuter rail vehicles in service since 2011 across the world. Stats vary so this is an average.

name: Leonardo
desc: The Leonardo is a metro EMU built by AnsaldoBreda for the Milan Metro's manual lines that entered service in 2009. Leonardo is likely inspired by AnsaldoBreda's parent company at the time, Leonardo SpA. Acceleration and cost is a guessimate.

name: Litra SA/SE (CPH)
desc: The Litra SA/SE is an EMU produced by Alstom and Siemens for the Copenhagen S-Tog system which entered service in 1996.

name: M Sets (SYD)
desc: The M sets / Millenniums are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by EDi Rail that entered service in 2002. Cost and acceleration are guesstimates.

name: M200 (HEL)
desc: The M200 is a metro EMU built for the Helsinki Metro by Bombardier and Alstom that entered service in the early 2000s. This is the version constrained by IRL platform size.

name: M200L (HEL)
desc: The M200 is a metro EMU built for the Helsinki Metro by Bombardier and Alstom that entered service in the early 2000s. This is the version constrained by the train's technology itself.

name: M300 (HEL)
desc: The M300 is a metro EMU built for the Helsinki Metro by CAF that entered service in 2016. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: M4 (AMS)
desc: The M4 is a tram and metro EMU built by CAF for the Amsterdam Metro in 2019. The original designation was S3 and they were built in 1996, but after being renovated they were renamed to M4 due to the significant changes, inclduing permanently extended steps for the wider loading gauge. Cost is an estimate.

name: M400 (HEL)
desc: The M400 is a metro EMU planned for the Helsinki Metro. Many numbers are estimated from existing models since this train hasn't even been contracted out yet.

name: M5 (AMS)
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. The M5 is a variant produced for the Amsterdam Metro.

name: M7 (AMS)
desc: The M4 is a metro EMU built by CAF for the Amsterdam Metro that entered service in 2023.

name: M7 (NYC)
desc: The M7 is a commuter rail EMU built by Bombardier for the MTA's LIRR and MNCR that entered service in 2011. Cost is an estimate.

name: M8 (NYC)
desc: The M8 is a commuter rail EMU built by Kawasaki for the Metro-North Railroad that entered service in 2011.

name: M9 (LIRR)
desc: The M9 is a commuter rail EMU built by Kawasaki for the Long Island Railroad that entered service in 2009.

name: Mark III (VAN)
desc: The Innovia Metro is an automated rapid transit system family built by Alstom/Bombardier that have been in service across North America since 1985. This specific model is based on the Mark III used in Vancouver since 2016. Acceleration and decceleration are guesstimates.

name: Mark V (VAN)
desc: The Innovia Metro is an automated rapid transit system family built by Alstom/Bombardier that have been in service across North America since 1985. This specific model is based on the Mark V used in Vancouver since 2025. Acceleration and decceleration are guesstimates.

name: MBTA Commuter Rail
desc: Honestly I cannot come up with a good summary of these things so just know its whatever Boston uses for their rail. Acceleration is guesstimated. Cost is an estimate.

name: Meneghino
desc: The Meneghino is a metro EMU built by AnsaldoBreda for the Milan Metro's manual lines that entered service in 2009. Meneghino derives from the acronym MNG (Metro New Generation). Meneghino is the name of the typical mask of the Milanese carnival. Acceleration and cost is a guessimate.

name: Metro E (SAO)
desc: The Metro E Series is a subway EMU built by Alstom for Sao Paulo that entered service in 1999.

name: Metro F (SAO)
desc: The Metro F Series is a subway EMU built by Alstom, CAF, and Siemens for Sao Paulo that entered service in 2001.

name: Metro G (SAO)
desc: The Metro G Series is a subway EMU built by Alstom for Sao Paulo that entered service in 2009.

name: Metro H (SAO)
desc: The Metro H Series is a subway EMU built by CAF for Sao Paulo that entered service in 2010.

name: Metro I (SAO)
desc: The Metro I Series is a subway EMU built by Mafersa for Sao Paulo that entered service in 1974 and was modernized by Alstom and Siemens from 2010-2018.

name: Metro J (SAO)
desc: The Metro J Series is a subway EMU built by Mafersa for Sao Paulo that entered service in 1974 and was modernized by Bombardier from 2011-2018.

name: Metro K (SAO)
desc: The Metro K Series is a subway EMU built by Cobrasma for Sao Paulo that entered service in 1974 and was modernized by MTTrens from 2010-2014.

name: Metro L (SAO)
desc: The Metro L Series is a subway EMU built by Mafersa for Sao Paulo that entered service in 1984 and was modernized by Alstom and IESA from 2011-2017.

name: Metro P (SAO)
desc: The Metro P Series is a subway EMU built by CAF for Sao Paulo that entered service in 2017.

name: Metropolis (SYD)
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. This is a fully automated variant for the Sydney Metro that entered service in 2019. They are expandable from 6 to 8 cars.

name: Metropolis C751A/C
desc: The C751A is an EMU built by Alstom for Singapore's MRT that entered service in 2003. They are based on Alstom's Metropolis family, and serve the North East Line. The C is identical but built as a joint venture with Shanghai Electric.

name: Metropolis C830(C)
desc: The C830 is an EMU built by Alstom for Singapore's MRT that entered service in 2006. They are based on Alstom's Metropolis family, and serve the Circle Line. The C830C is a later variant built in partnership with Shanghai Electric.

name: Metropolis C851E
desc: The C851E is an EMU built by Alstom for Singapore's MRT that entered service in 2023. They are based on Alstom's Metropolis family, and serve the North East Line as 6 car sets. They will soon enter service in the Circle Line as 3 car sets.

name: MF 01 (PAR)
desc: The MF 01 is a steel-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family.  It entered service in 2008.

name: MF 19 (PAR)
desc: The MF 19 is a steel-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. This is the standard version which comes in either 4 or 5 car sets. It entered service in 2025. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: MF 19 L (PAR)
desc: The MF 19 is a steel-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. This is the slightly longer version which comes in 5 car sets. It will enter service in 2027. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: MF 19 L CA (PAR)
desc: The MF 19 is a steel-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. This is the automated version which will be deployed in the 2030s once Line 13 is automated. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: MFAV
desc: The MHAV is an electric vehicle built by Ganz-MAVAG and Ganz Electric for the Budapest Metro M1 that entered service in 1973. It honestly is closer to an LRT than most metro cars. Cost is an estimate.

name: MI 09 (PAR)
desc: The MI 09 is a double-decker EMU built by Alstom/Bombardier as part of their X'Trapolis family that has been in service on the French RER's Line A since 2011.

name: MI 20 (PAR)
desc: The MI 20 is a mixed single-double decker EMU built by Alstom/Bombardier and CAF that will enter service on the French RER's line B in 2029. Width and acceleration taken from previous models due to lack of info.

name: MI 79/84 (PAR)
desc: The MI 79/84 is an EMU built by Alstom and SFB that has been in service on the French RER's Line B since 1981. Cost is an estimate.

name: Movia C951
desc: The C951 is an EMU built by Bombardier/Alstom and CRRC for Singapore's MRT that entered service in 2013. They serve on the Downtown Line. They are based on the ADTranz/Bombardier/Alstom Movia family.

name: Movia R141
desc: The R141 is an EMU built by Alstom/Bombardier and CRRC for Singapore's MRT that entered service in 2023. They serve on the North-South and East-West lines. They are based on the ADTranz/Bombardier/Alstom Movia family.

name: MP 05 (PAR)
desc: The MP 05 is an automated rubber-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. It entered service in 2011.

name: MP 14 CA (PAR)
desc: The MP 14 CA is an automated rubber-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. It entered service in 2020.

name: MP 14 CC (PAR)
desc: The MP 14 CC is a rubber-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. It entered service in 2023.

name: MP 89 CA (PAR)
desc: The MP 89 CA is an automated rubber-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. It entered service in 1998. Cost is an estimate.

name: MP 89 CC (PAR)
desc: The MP 89 CC is a rubber-wheeled subway EMU produced by Alstom for the Paris Metro based on the Metropolis family. It entered service in 1997. Cost is an estimate.

name: MPX-6000 Series
desc: The MPI MPXpress is a line of diesel locomotives that first entered service in 2003. This specific trainset carries passengers with Metra's 6000 series cars, which are bilevel coaches built by Nippon Sharyo.

name: MPX-BBLC
desc: The MPI MPXpress is a line of diesel locomotives that first entered service in 2003. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier BiLevel Coaches.

name: MPX-VRE
desc: The MPI MPXpress is a line of diesel locomotives that first entered service in 2003. This specific trainset carries passengers with the Virginia Rail Express's nameless cars from Sumitomo/Nippon Sharyo. Cost is an estimate.

name: MR-73 (MTL)
desc: The MR-73 is a rubber-tire heavy metro EMU built by Bombardier for the Montreal Metro that entered service in 1976. Cost is an estimate.

name: MR3V/MR6V (PAR)
desc: The MR3V/MR6V is an automated steel-wheeled subway EMU being developed by Alstom for the Paris Metro's future Lines 15-17 based on the Metropolis family. These three lines will built to more modern standards. Some numbers are guesstimated since this set is not out yet.

name: MRV (PAR)
desc: The MRV is an automated steel-wheeled subway EMU being developed by Alstom for the Paris Metro's future Line 18 based on the Metropolis family. It has unique electrification. Some numbers are guesstimated since this set is not out yet.

name: MultiLevel Coach III EMU
desc: The MultiLevel Coach III is a commuter rail EMU built by Bombardier/Alstom that entered service in 2006. They are used on multiple networks across Eastern North America.

name: N Sets (NSW)
desc: The N sets / Endeavours are commuter rail DMUs built for Sydney Trains by ABB that entered service in 1994. Acceleration and cost are guesstimates. Capacity is slightly increased due to game limits.

name: NET 2012
desc: The NET 2012 is a variant of the Vossloh/Stadler Citylink LRV that was built for Karlsruhe that entered service in 2014.

name: Nippon Sharyo DMU
desc: The Nippon Sharyo DMU is an acutely named vehicle which has been in service since 2015 in California and Toronto.

name: NJT LRV-3
desc: This is an unnamed articulated low-floor light-rail vehicle built by Kinki-Sharyo for New Jersey Transit, specifically Hudson-Bergen Light Rail and Newark Light Rail. It has been in service since 2000. This is the original, 3 section version.

name: NJT LRV-5
desc: This is an unnamed articulated low-floor light-rail vehicle built by Kinki-Sharyo for New Jersey Transit, specifically Hudson-Bergen Light Rail and Newark Light Rail. It has been in service since 2000. This is the 5 section version.

name: NM-16 (MXC)
desc: The NM-16 is a rubber-tyred model of EMU built by CAF used on the Mexico City Metro

name: NST (TOR)
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. The NST is a heavy metro variant built for the Toronto Subway that was orded in January 2026. Many numbers are guesstimates since the train doesn't really exist fully yet.

name: OL Series 14 (BOS)
desc: The 14th Series of rolling stock for the MBTA Orange Line is an EMU built by CRRC from 2018-2025. Acceleration and capacity is guessimated.

name: P3010 LRV (LA)
desc: The P3010 is an articulated low-floor light-rail vehicle built by Kinki Sharyo that has been in service in LA since 2016.

name: P3010 LRV (LA) (C)
desc: The P3010 is an articulated low-floor light-rail vehicle built by Kinki Sharyo that has been in service in LA since 2016. This is the line C version which, as it is fully grade separated, is at GoA2.

name: PA5
desc: The PA-5 is a metro EMU built by Kawasaki for the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey and its PATH service that entered service in 2009. They are a derivative of the R142As ordered by the MTA for the NYC Subway.

name: Prima-VB2N
desc: The BB 27300 is an electric passenger locomotive built by Alstom as a derivative of 27000 Prima Family that entered service in 2006. This trainset carries passengers with VB 2N double-decker cars. It is currently being used on Transilien's Line J. Acceleration is an estimate. Cost is an estimate

name: R Sets (NSW)
desc: The R sets are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by CAF based on the Civity that will enter service by 2029. Acceleration and capacity are guesstimates.

name: R142 (NYC)
desc: The R142 is a subway EMU built by Bombardier (now Alstom) and Kawsak ifor the NYC Subway's A Division that entered service in 2000. As of 2024, they are deployed on lines 2, 4, and 5.

name: R143 (NYC)
desc: The R143 is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the B Division of the NYC Subway that entered service in 2001. As of 2024, they serve line L.

name: R160A (NYC)
desc: The R160A is a subway EMU built by Alstom for the NYC Subway's B Divison that entered service in 2006. It is effectively identical to the R160B except in manufacturer, and that the As come in 4 or 5 cars while the Bs come in 5 cars only. As of 2024, they serve lines J/Z,L, and M.

name: R160B (NYC)
desc: The R160B is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the NYC Subway's B Divison that entered service in 2006. It is effectively identical to the R160A except in manufacturer, and that the As come in 4 or 5 cars while the Bs come in 5 cars only. As of 2024, they serve lines E,F, and R.

name: R179 (NYC)
desc: The R179 is a subway EMU built by Bombardier (now Alstom) for the NYC Subway's B Division that entered service in 2019.

name: R188-11 (NYC)
desc: The R188 is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the NYC Subway's A Division that entered service in 2013. This is the 11 car variant which is made up of a 5 car and a 6 car set. It can also handle 10 cars if you use a 5 and a 5. 11 cars is what is used IRL. As of 2024, they are deployed on the 7 Line.

name: R188-5 (NYC)
desc: The R188 is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the NYC Subway's A Division that entered service in 2013. This is the 5/10 car variant. As of 2024, they are deployed on the 7 Line.

name: R188-6 (NYC)
desc: The R188 is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the NYC Subway's A Division that entered service in 2013. This is the 6/12 car variant. 12 cars is not used IRL, but they can couple 2 5 car sets and a 5 car and a 6 car so they can most likely do a 6 and a 6 if the stations were long enough. As of 2024, they are deployed on the 7 Line.

name: R211 (NYC)
desc: The R211 is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the B Division of the NYC Subway that entered service in 2023. As of 2024, they are deployed on the A,B,C,G, and Rockaway Shuttle Lines.

name: R211S (NYC)
desc: The R211S is an EMU built by Kawasaki for the Staten Island Railway that entered service in 2023 and replaced the very old R44s. The SIR is why the tag "Weird Metro" was created, since it's basically a subway and the interior is that of a subway car, but it has level crossings. Like legitimately this is the same thing as the regular R211, just with the third rail contact moved slightly since the SIR has a different height of third rail.

name: R262-5 (NYC)
desc: The R262 is a subway EMU in development for the A Division of the NYC Subway to enter service in the 2030s. This is the 5 car set version for the IRT. They will replace the R62s and R62As. Some data is assumed from prior models.

name: R262-6 (NYC)
desc: The R262 is a subway EMU in development for the A Division of the NYC Subway to enter service in the 2030s. This is the 6 car set version for the 42nd Street Shuttle, which will only use them in sets of 1 but 2 is theoretically possible. They will replace the R62s and R62As. Some data is assumed from prior models.

name: R268-4 (NYC)
desc: The R268 is a subway EMU being built by Kawasaki for the B Division of the NYC Subway to enter service in 2028. This is the 4-car set version. They will replace the R68s and R68As.

name: R268-5 (NYC)
desc: The R268 is a subway EMU being built by Kawasaki for the B Division of the NYC Subway to enter service in 2028. This is the 5-car set version. They will replace the R68s and R68As.

name: R62 (NYC)
desc: The R62 is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the A Division of the NYC Subway that entered service in 1983. It was originally built as single cars but renovated to be 5 car sets. As of 2024, they serve lines 1, 3, 6.

name: R62A (NYC)
desc: The R62A is a subway EMU built by Bombardier for the A Division of the NYC Subway that entered service in 1985. They are the same design as the R62, but since Kawasaki didn't want to build more, Bombardier did it. It was originally built as single cars but renovated to be 5 and 6 car sets. The 6 car sets serve the 42nd Street Shuttle as single set trains, but since they are capable of being run as 12 cars if the station were long enough, they have been enabled for this mod. As of 2024, they serve the 42nd Street Shuttle.

name: R68 (NYC)
desc: The R68 is a subway EMU built by Westinghouse-Amrail aka Francorail aka Westinghouse, ANF, Jeumont Schneider, and Alstom for the B Division of the NYC Subway that entered service in 1986. To quote Wikipedia, "the fleet became known as a lemon in its early years". Most are linked in 4 car sets after being built as single units, but since they are used in revenue service as coupled single units as low as 2 cars, a full range of lengths has been made available. As of 2024, they serve lines B, D, N, Q, W, and the Franklin Avenue Shuttle.

name: R68A (NYC)
desc: The R68A is a subway EMU built by Kawasaki for the NYC Subway's B Division that entered service in 1988. Unlike the regular R68, these were built as 4 car sets only. As of 2024, they serve lines B, D, N, Q, and W.

name: Regio 2N Paris
desc: The Regio 2N is a family of double-decker EMUs built by Alstom/Bombardier that have been in service since 2013. This variant is the Transilien's Z 57000, which serves lines D, N, and R.

name: RL Series 4 (BOS)
desc: The 4th Series of rolling stock for the MBTA Red Line is a subway EMU built by CRRC. Acceleration is guessimated.

name: Rotem EMU (VAN)
desc: The Rotem is an automated EMU built by Hyundai for the Vancouver Skyline's Canada Line that entered service in 2009.

name: S Stock (LDN)
desc: S Stock is a subway EMU built by Bombardier for London's Subsurface Tube, that comes in either 7 or 8 car sets. S8s operate on the Metropolitan line, while S7s are on the Circle, District, and Hammersmith & City Lines.

name: S-Tog Gen 5
desc: The currently unnamed 5th Generation of rolling stock on the Copenhagen S-Tog is being designed by a Siemens-Stadler consortium. They will be fully automatic S-Tog / S-Bahn EMUs and are supposed to enter service in 2032.

name: S/300 (ROM)
desc: The S/300 is a metro EMU built by CAF for the Rome Metro's Lines A and B that has been in service since 2005. Cost is an estimate.

name: S200-HF (CGY)
desc: The S200 series are articulated high-floor light-rail vehicles built by Siemens that have been in service across North America since 2016. This specific model is based on the model used in Calgary since 2019. Capacity is artificially higher due to game processing requirements.

name: S200-HF (SF)
desc: The S200 series are articulated high-floor light-rail vehicles built by Siemens that have been in service across North America since 2016. This specific model is based on the model used in San Francisco since 2017. Capacity is artificially higher due to game processing requirements.

name: S70 Streetcar
desc: The S700 Streetcar is an articulated low-floor streetcar built by Siemens that have been in service across North America since 2004. This specific model is based on the streetcar model ordered by OC Streetcar. Capacity is artificially higher due to game processing requirements.

name: S700
desc: The S700 series are articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Siemens that have been in service across North America since 2004. This specific model is based on those recieved in 2020 by Metro Transit in Minnesota.

name: S700-US
desc: The S700 series are articulated low-floor light-rail vehicles built by Siemens that have been in service across North America since 2004. This specific model is based on the ultra-short model used in San Diego.

name: Saint-Laurent (MTL)
desc: The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. The Saint-Laurent is an autonomous light metro variant built for the Réseau express métropolitain in Montreal that entered service in 2023. Cost is a estimate based on the overall contract Alstom signed which includes signalling and stuff

name: SC-BBLC
desc: The Siemens Charger is a family of diesel locomotives that first entered service in 2017. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier BiLevel Coach (aka Adessia Coach) cars, as used by SF's ACE.

name: SC-BMLC
desc: The Siemens Charger is a family of diesel locomotives that first entered service in 2017. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier MultiLevel Coach (aka Adessia Coach) cars, as used by MARC.

name: SC-SL4
desc: The Siemens Charger is a family of diesel locomotives that first entered service in 2017. This specific trainset carries passengers with Bombardier Shoreliner IVs, as used by MNCR.

name: SD-100/160
desc: The SD-100/160 series are articulated high-floor light-rail vehicles built by Siemens that have been in service across North America since 1994. Capacity slightly increased due to game processing requirements.

name: Seattle Series 1
desc: The Series 1 is an articulated low-floor light-rail vehicle built by Kinki Sharyo that has been in service in Seattle since 2009. Cost is an estimate.

name: SEPTA BIV
desc: The Kawasaki BIV Subway Car is an EMU used on the B line of the Philadelphia Subway. Many of these stats are estimates due to lack of data.

name: Series 112 (BCN)
desc: The Series 112 is a metro EMU built by Alstom, CAF, and ABB which enterered service on the Barcelona Metro in 1995. Acceleration is guesstimated.

name: Series 113 (BCN)
desc: The Series 113 is a metro EMU built by Alstom and CAF which enterered service on the Barcelona Metro in 2014.

name: Series 114 (BCN)
desc: The Series 114 is a metro EMU built by Alstom and CAF which enterered service on the Barcelona Metro in 2014.

name: Series 115 (BCN)
desc: The Series 115 is a metro EMU built by Stadler which enterered service on the Barcelona Metro in 2022. Acceleration is guesstimated.

name: Series 2070 (SAO)
desc: The Series 2070 is a commuter rail EMU built by Alstom, Bombardier, and CAF for Sao Paulo that entered service in 2008.

name: Series 2100 (BCN)
desc: The Series 2100 is a heavy metro EMU built by CAF, Meinfesa, and Alstom for the Barcelona metro that entered service in 1995. They were renovated in 2019. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: Series 213 (BCN)

name: VDV-Tram-Train

name: ViaQuatro Hyundai EMU
desc: The ViaQuatro Hyundai EMU is a vehicle without a formal name which entered service in 2009. Acceleration is a guesstimate.

name: VR Class Sm5

name: VR Class Sm7
desc: The VR Class Sm7 is a Stadler FLIRT-based commuter rail EMU built for usage in Finland. They are significantly wider than the average FLIRT and are broad gauge. They will enter service in 2026.

name: Z 20500

name: Z 20900

name: Z 5600

name: Z 58000

name: Z 58500

name: Z 8800
